title: Add delete profile and delete content options

requirements.text: Add a 'Delete Profile' option to the user's profile area that allows an authenticated user to permanently delete their profile from the platform. The action must prompt the user with a confirmation dialog before proceeding. Upon successful deletion, the user should be logged out and redirected to the feed page.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Delete Profile' button is visible to the authenticated user in the profile area.
Clicking 'Delete Profile' opens a confirmation dialog warning the user that the action is irreversible.
Confirming deletion calls the backend to remove the user's profile data.
After successful deletion, the user is logged out and redirected to the feed page.
The backend exposes a deleteProfile endpoint that removes the caller's profile record.

requirements.text: Add an optional 'Delete Content' button to each content item that the authenticated user owns. The button should only be visible to the content owner. Clicking it must show a confirmation dialog before permanently deleting the content item. After deletion, the content item should be removed from the feed.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Delete Content' button is rendered on content cards and/or the content detail page only when the authenticated user is the content owner.
Clicking 'Delete Content' opens a confirmation dialog before any deletion occurs.
Confirming deletion calls the backend to permanently remove the content item.
After successful deletion, the content is removed from the feed and any related UI (e.g., queue, detail page) without requiring a full page reload.
The backend exposes a deleteContent endpoint that accepts a content ID and only allows deletion by the content owner.

constraints: Delete profile and delete content actions must only be available to the authenticated content/profile owner.
Both destructive actions must require explicit user confirmation before executing.
Do not edit any files listed under frontend.immutablePaths.
nonGoals: Bulk deletion of multiple content items at once.
Admin-level deletion of other users' profiles or content.
Soft-delete or archiving — deletions are permanent.
